sections.paragraph:
i.q. Ch. שְׁרָא to loose.

Piel, שֵׁרָה to loose, Jer 15:11 קרי, שֵׁרִיתְךָ לְטֹוב “I will loose thee for good,” i.e. I will set thee free. The Hebrews appear to have used this verb also in a bad sense (לְרַע), for to desert, on which account there is added in this place לְטֹוב.

Derivatives, מִשְׁרַה, שֵׁרוּת.

II.

שרה

perhaps i.q. Arab. شرى to shine, to glitter (as lightning); hence שִׁרְיָה, שִׁרְיֹון a coat of mail.
